Group member: Oliver Nielikäinen, Jaakko Nurminen, Aarni Kivelä

Seizure detection from EEG data¶

Preview into dataset¶

EEG signals have been collected from recordings from 10 different subjects, in total there are 739 EEG recordings (i.e. files). The number of recordings between subjects varies, but each recording is 10 seconds long time window. Recordings are further devided into "seizure" and "nonseizure" subsets. "seizure" subset has 339 EEG signals with seizures. Similarly "non-seizure" subset has 400 records of normal EEG signal. Each record is saved as CSV and has 23 columns that correspond to 23 unique EEG channels. The filename includes the event number and the subject number. For example, 0 is the event number, and 01 is the subject number in “sample_chb01_0.csv.” Note that the subjects have both the “nonseizure” and “seizure” events.

Overview¶

  • Total recordings: 739
  • Subjects: 10
  • Recording duration: 10 seconds each
  • File format: CSV with 23 columns (EEG channels)

Subsets¶

Subset Count Description
Seizure 339 EEG recordings with seizure events
Non-seizure 400 Normal EEG recordings
Total 739

Hierarchy¶

EEG Dataset (739 recordings · 10 subjects)
│
├── Subject 01
│   ├── Seizure recordings
│   └── Non-seizure recordings
│
├── Subject 02
│   ├── Seizure recordings
│   └── Non-seizure recordings
│
├── · · ·
│
└── Subject 10
    ├── Seizure recordings
    └── Non-seizure recordings

Recording (CSV File)¶

  • Duration: 10-second time window
  • Columns: 23 (one per EEG channel)
  • Filename format: sample_chb[subj]_[event].csv
  • Example: sample_chb01_0.csv → subject 01, event 0

Setup¶

import os
from collections import defaultdict
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np 
import glob
from pprint import pp

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

seizure = defaultdict(list)
nonseizure = defaultdict(list)

seizure_data = glob.glob("data/seizure/*.csv")
nonseizure_data = glob.glob("data/nonseizure/*.csv")
In [4]:
def getdata(path, dictionary):
    for file in path:
        df = pd.read_csv(file)
        dictionary[file].append(df)

getdata(seizure_data, seizure)
getdata(nonseizure_data, nonseizure)

seizure_keys = seizure.keys()
nonseizure_keys = nonseizure.keys()
for path, data in list(seizure.items())[:2]:
    display(data)

Visualize data¶

In [6]:
# Extract patient numbers from file paths
def get_patient_number(filepath):
    """Extract patient number from filepath like 'data/seizure/sample_chb01_0.csv'"""
    filename = filepath.split('/')[-1]  # Get 'sample_chb01_0.csv'
    patient_num = filename.split('chb')[1][:2]  # Extract '01'
    return patient_num


def plot_overview(seizure_data, nonseizure_data, num_patients=3):
    # Get unique patients
    seizure_patients = {}
    nonseizure_patients = {}

    for path in seizure_data.keys():
        patient = get_patient_number(path)
        if patient not in seizure_patients:
            seizure_patients[patient] = path

    for path in nonseizure_data.keys():
        patient = get_patient_number(path)
        if patient not in nonseizure_patients:
            nonseizure_patients[patient] = path

    # Select first num_patients patients
    selected_patients = sorted(seizure_patients.keys())[:num_patients]

    # Create subplots: 2 rows (seizure and non-seizure), num_patients columns
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, num_patients, figsize=(5*num_patients, 10))

    # Plot seizure signals in top row
    for idx, patient in enumerate(selected_patients):
        ax = axes[0, idx]
        path = seizure_patients[patient]
        df = seizure_data[path][0]
        
        # Plot all 23 channels
        for channel in df.columns:
            ax.plot(df[channel], alpha=0.7, linewidth=0.8)
        
        ax.set_title(f'Seizure - Patient {patient}', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
        ax.set_xlabel('Sample')
        ax.set_ylabel('Amplitude (μV)')
        ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    # Plot non-seizure signals in bottom row
    for idx, patient in enumerate(selected_patients):
        ax = axes[1, idx]
        path = nonseizure_patients[patient]
        df = nonseizure_data[path][0]
        
        # Plot all 23 channels
        for channel in df.columns:
            ax.plot(df[channel], alpha=0.7, linewidth=0.8)
        
        ax.set_title(f'Non-Seizure - Patient {patient}', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
        ax.set_xlabel('Sample')
        ax.set_ylabel('Amplitude (μV)')
        ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()


# Call the function with default num_patients=3
plot_overview(seizure, nonseizure, num_patients=3)


def plot_patient_channels(seizure_data, nonseizure_data, patient_num):
    seizure_patients = {}
    nonseizure_patients = {}

    for path in seizure_data.keys():
        patient = get_patient_number(path)
        if patient not in seizure_patients:
            seizure_patients[patient] = path

    for path in nonseizure_data.keys():
        patient = get_patient_number(path)
        if patient not in nonseizure_patients:
            nonseizure_patients[patient] = path
    
    seizure_path = seizure_patients[patient_num]
    nonseizure_path = nonseizure_patients[patient_num]
    seizure_df = seizure_data[seizure_path][0]
    nonseizure_df = nonseizure_data[nonseizure_path][0]

    # Plot channels
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(6, 4, figsize=(18, 12), layout='constrained')
    axes = axes.flatten()

    fig.suptitle(f"Channels - Patient {patient_num}", fontsize=16, fontweight='bold')
    fig.supxlabel('Sample', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
    fig.supylabel('Amplitude (μV)', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')



    # Plot all 23 channels
    for i, (seiz_ch, nonseiz_ch) in enumerate(zip(seizure_df.columns, nonseizure_df.columns)):
        axes[i].plot(nonseizure_df[nonseiz_ch], alpha=0.7, linewidth=0.8, color='green')
        axes[i].plot(seizure_df[seiz_ch], alpha=0.7, linewidth=0.8, color='red')

        axes[i].set_title(f'Ch: {seiz_ch}', fontsize=12)
        axes[i].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

    # remove empty subplot
    fig.delaxes(axes[-1])


    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

plot_patient_channels(seizure, nonseizure, '02')

C:\Users\olive\AppData\Local\Temp\ipykernel_21256\1293014132.py:109: UserWarning: The figure layout has changed to tight
  plt.tight_layout()

We use two different classifiers, random forest (rf) and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). They require the data in different shape so their preprocessing is done differently. On the preprocessing that is done on CNN, 3-level wavelet decomposition is done for each EEG segment. The resulting coefficient arrays are concatenated and that concatenated vector is z-score normalized. All channels are stacked to produce a 2D array of shape (dwt_len, n_channels), which becomes one sample for the model. The train/test split is done per-patient with a hard 70% cutoff by list index. Also a leave-one-patient-out cross-validation is done.

In the preprocessing of the rf, per segment we standardize each channel and then do the dwt decomposition using db3 at level 3 with periodization, which produces the coefficients and concatenates them into a 1D array per channel. Then the different kind of features are extracted from the segments

  • wavelet features(mean absolute value, std, and energy for each of A3/D3/D2/D1 per channel — 12 values per channel)
  • band power features (delta, theta, alpha, beta — 4 values per channel)
  • time-domain features (mean, std, max absolute value, skewness — 4 values per channel)

Thats total of 20 features per channel and a flat vector with n_channels x 20 features is computed for each segment. The train/test split is done at the patient level in the way that no patient appears in both sets. This gives us more realistic results about the prediction performance on unseen patients since the model can't learn to predict patient, while now it actually predicts the seizure/non-seizure classes.

2D CNN¶

Preprocessing¶

  1. Do Discrete Wavelet Transform for EEG channels so that we can create signal vector that we will later on feed to the model.
In [7]:
import pywt
from scipy.stats import zscore

# Do wavelet transform for every patients every channel
# Concatenate these together to create 1d array

def process_segment_2d(segment_df, wavelet='db1', level=3):
    channel_features = []
    for col in segment_df.columns:
        coeffs = pywt.wavedec(segment_df[col].values, wavelet, level=level)
        normalized_channels = zscore(np.concatenate(coeffs))
        channel_features.append(normalized_channels)
    return np.stack(channel_features, axis=-1)  # (dwt_len, n_channels)


def aggregate_patient_signals(seizure_data, non_seizure_data):
    """
    return wavelet transformed signals in dict:

    signals: {
        patient_1: 
            seizure: [s1, s2, s3, ..., sn]
            non-seizure: [s1, s2, s3, ..., sn]
        patient_2:
            seizure: [s1, s2, s3, ..., sn]
            non-seizure: [s1, s2, s3, ..., sn]
        patient_3:
            seizure: [s1, s2, s3, ..., sn]
            non-seizure: [s1, s2, s3, ..., sn]
        ...
        patient_n:
            seizure: [s1, s2, s3, ..., sn]
            non-seizure: [s1, s2, s3, ..., sn]
    }
    """

    patients = []

    # initiate patients list
    for path in seizure_data:
        patient_id = get_patient_number(path) # '01'
        if patient_id not in patients:
            patients.append(patient_id)

    patients.sort()

    signals = {p: {'seizure': [], 'non-seizure': []} for p in patients}

    # append data for patient
    for path in seizure_data:
        patient_id = get_patient_number(path)
        signal_df = seizure_data[path][0] # unwrap df from list

        signals[patient_id]['seizure'].append(signal_df)

    for path in non_seizure_data:
        patient_id = get_patient_number(path)
        signal_df = non_seizure_data[path][0] # unwrap df from list

        signals[patient_id]['non-seizure'].append(signal_df)

    for k, v in signals.items():
        print(f"{k}:\n\tseizure: {len(v['seizure'])}\n\tnon-seizure: {len(v['non-seizure'])}")

    display(signals['01']['seizure'][0])

    return signals


patient_signals = aggregate_patient_signals(seizure, nonseizure)


X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = [], [], [], []

for patient_id, segments in patient_signals.items():
    seizure_segments = segments['seizure']
    non_seizure_segments = segments['non-seizure']

    n_sz_train = int(0.7 * len(seizure_segments))
    n_ns_train = int(0.7 * len(non_seizure_segments))

    for seg in seizure_segments[:n_sz_train]:
        X_train.append(process_segment_2d(seg))
        y_train.append(1)
    for seg in seizure_segments[n_sz_train:]:
        X_test.append(process_segment_2d(seg))
        y_test.append(1)
    for seg in non_seizure_segments[:n_ns_train]:
        X_train.append(process_segment_2d(seg))
        y_train.append(0)
    for seg in non_seizure_segments[n_ns_train:]:
        X_test.append(process_segment_2d(seg))
        y_test.append(0)

X_train = np.array(X_train)  # (512, 2560, n_channels)
X_test  = np.array(X_test)
y_train = np.array(y_train)
y_test  = np.array(y_test)

print(f"Mean: {X_train[0].mean()}")
print(f"Std: {X_train[0].std()}")

print(X_train.shape)  # expect (512, 2560, n_channels)

In [8]:
# Build model
from tensorflow import keras
from tensorflow.keras import layers, models, optimizers
from tensorflow.keras.regularizers import L2

# Add this to detect the issue:
print(f"X_train has NaN: {np.isnan(X_train).any()}")
print(f"X_train has Inf: {np.isinf(X_train).any()}")
print(f"X_train min: {X_train.min()}, max: {X_train.max()}")

def build_model(input_shape):
    model = models.Sequential()

    model.add(layers.Conv1D(16, 4, strides=4, padding='valid', input_shape=input_shape))
    model.add(layers.BatchNormalization())
    model.add(layers.Activation('relu'))

    model.add(layers.Conv1D(32, 4, strides=2, padding='valid'))
    model.add(layers.BatchNormalization())
    model.add(layers.Activation('relu'))
    model.add(layers.MaxPooling1D(3, 2))

    model.add(layers.Conv1D(64, 3, strides=2, padding='valid'))
    model.add(layers.BatchNormalization())
    model.add(layers.Activation('relu'))
    model.add(layers.MaxPooling1D(3, 2))

    model.add(layers.LSTM(32, dropout=0.3, recurrent_dropout=0.2))

    model.add(layers.Dense(32, activation='relu', kernel_regularizer=L2(0.05)))
    model.add(layers.Dropout(0.5))
    model.add(layers.Dense(1, activation='sigmoid'))

    model.compile(
        optimizer=optimizers.Adam(learning_rate=0.0001),
        loss='binary_crossentropy',
        metrics=['accuracy']
    )

    return model

model = build_model((X_train.shape[1], X_train.shape[2]))
model.summary()

In [10]:
history = model.fit(
    X_train, y_train,
    epochs=300,
    batch_size=40,
    validation_data=(X_test, y_test),
    verbose=1
)

val_acc = max(history.history['val_accuracy'])
print(f"  → val_accuracy: {val_acc:.4f}")

from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score, precision_score, recall_score, f1_score, confusion_matrix, ConfusionMatrixDisplay

acc = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred)
precision = precision_score(y_test, y_pred)
recall = recall_score(y_test, y_pred)
f1 = f1_score(y_test, y_pred)
cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred)

print(f"Accuracy:  {acc:.4f}")
print(f"Precision: {precision:.4f}")
print(f"Recall:    {recall:.4f}")
print(f"F1-score:  {f1:.4f}")
print("Confusion Matrix:")
disp = ConfusionMatrixDisplay(confusion_matrix=cm, display_labels=["Non-seizure", "Seizure"])
disp.plot()
plt.show()

In [13]:
# Build full dataset keeping patient labels
X_by_patient = {}
y_by_patient = {}

for patient_id, segments in patient_signals.items():
    X_patient = []
    y_patient = []
    for seg in segments['seizure']:
        X_patient.append(process_segment_2d(seg))
        y_patient.append(1)
    for seg in segments['non-seizure']:
        X_patient.append(process_segment_2d(seg))
        y_patient.append(0)
    X_by_patient[patient_id] = np.array(X_patient)
    y_by_patient[patient_id] = np.array(y_patient)

patient_ids = list(X_by_patient.keys())
fold_results = []

for i, test_patient in enumerate(patient_ids):
    print(f"\nFold {i+1}/{len(patient_ids)} — test patient: {test_patient}")

    # Build train set from all other patients
    train_patients = [p for p in patient_ids if p != test_patient]
    X_train = np.concatenate([X_by_patient[p] for p in train_patients])
    y_train = np.concatenate([y_by_patient[p] for p in train_patients])
    X_test  = X_by_patient[test_patient]
    y_test  = y_by_patient[test_patient]

    print(f"  Train: {X_train.shape}, Test: {X_test.shape}")

    # Reset model each fold
    # keras.backend.clear_session()
    model = build_model((X_train.shape[1], X_train.shape[2]))

    history = model.fit(
        X_train, y_train,
        epochs=300,
        batch_size=32,
        validation_data=(X_test, y_test),
        verbose=0
    )

    best_val_acc = max(history.history['val_accuracy'])
    fold_results.append({'patient': test_patient, 'val_accuracy': best_val_acc})
    print(f"  → val_accuracy: {best_val_acc:.4f}")

# Summary
print("\nLOPO Results")
for r in fold_results:
    print(f"  {r['patient']}: {r['val_accuracy']:.4f}")
accs = [r['val_accuracy'] for r in fold_results]
print(f"\nMean: {np.mean(accs):.4f} ± {np.std(accs):.4f}")

Random Forest¶

Preprocessing¶

  • Do DWT decomposition, so we get the db3 coefficients
  • From coefficients, we can extract the features: wavelet features, band power and time-domain features into n_channels x 20 size featurevector
In [14]:
import pywt
from scipy.stats import zscore

# Do wavelet transform for every patients every channel
# Concatenate these together to create 1d array


def dwt_decompose(signal, wavelet='db3', level=3):
    """
    Perform multi-level DWT decomposition.
    Returns approximation (A3) and detail coefficients (D1, D2, D3).
    
    Level 1: D1
    Level 2: D2  
    Level 3: A3, D3
    """
    coeffs = pywt.wavedec(signal, wavelet=wavelet, level=level, mode='periodization')
    
    # coeffs = [A3, D3, D2, D1]  ← pywt returns highest approx first
    A3 = coeffs[0]
    D3 = coeffs[1]
    D2 = coeffs[2]
    D1 = coeffs[3]

    results = np.concatenate([A3, D3, D2, D1])

    return results

def safe_zscore(col):
    return col * 0 if col.std() == 0 else zscore(col)

def aggregate_patient_signals(seizure_data, non_seizure_data):
    patients = []
    for path in seizure_data:
        patient_id = get_patient_number(path)
        if patient_id not in patients:
            patients.append(patient_id)
    patients.sort()

    signals = {p: {'seizure': [], 'non-seizure': []} for p in patients}

    def process_segment(path, data_dict, label):
        patient_id = get_patient_number(path)
        signal_df = data_dict[path][0]
        normalized_df = signal_df.apply(safe_zscore)
        dwt_df = normalized_df.apply(dwt_decompose)

        has_nan = any(np.isnan(dwt_df[col].values).any() for col in dwt_df.columns)
        if has_nan:
            print(f"Skipping {path} — contains NaN after DWT")
            return

        signals[patient_id][label].append(dwt_df)

    for path in seizure_data:
        process_segment(path, seizure_data, 'seizure')

    for path in non_seizure_data:
        process_segment(path, non_seizure_data, 'non-seizure')

    for k, v in signals.items():
        print(f"{k}:\n\tseizure: {len(v['seizure'])}\n\tnon-seizure: {len(v['non-seizure'])}")

    return signals


patient_signals = aggregate_patient_signals(seizure, nonseizure)

	non-seizure: 40

	non-seizure: 40

	non-seizure: 40
10:
	seizure: 35
	non-seizure: 40
In [16]:
import numpy as np
import pywt
from scipy.stats import skew
from scipy.signal import welch
import pandas as pd

# Feature extraction

# using welch, signal power in a specific frequency band is computed
def bandpower(signal, fs, fmin, fmax):
    freqs, psd = welch(signal, fs=fs, nperseg=256)
    idx = np.logical_and(freqs >= fmin, freqs <= fmax)
    return np.trapz(psd[idx], freqs[idx])

# extracts the features from the given segment
def extract_features(segment_df, fs=256):
    features = []
    feature_names = []

    for col in segment_df.columns:
        signal = segment_df[col].values

        # Wavelet features 
        coeffs = pywt.wavedec(signal, 'db4', level=3)
        A3, D3, D2, D1 = coeffs

        for band_name, band in zip(['A3', 'D3', 'D2', 'D1'], [A3, D3, D2, D1]):
            features.extend([
                np.mean(np.abs(band)),
                np.std(band),
                np.sum(band ** 2),
            ])
            feature_names.extend([
                f"{col}_{band_name}_meanabs",
                f"{col}_{band_name}_std",
                f"{col}_{band_name}_energy",
            ])

        # Frequency features 
        features.extend([
            bandpower(signal, fs, 0.5, 4),
            bandpower(signal, fs, 4, 8),
            bandpower(signal, fs, 8, 13),
            bandpower(signal, fs, 13, 30),
        ])
        feature_names.extend([
            f"{col}_delta",
            f"{col}_theta",
            f"{col}_alpha",
            f"{col}_beta",
        ])

        #  Time-domain features 
        features.extend([
            np.mean(signal),
            np.std(signal),
            np.max(np.abs(signal)),
            skew(signal),
        ])
        feature_names.extend([
            f"{col}_mean",
            f"{col}_std",
            f"{col}_maxabs",
            f"{col}_skew",
        ])

    return np.array(features), feature_names


# Splitting the data into train/test with 70/30 split at patient level

patient_ids = list(patient_signals.keys())

patient_stats = []
for pid in patient_ids:
    seiz = len(patient_signals[pid]['seizure'])
    non  = len(patient_signals[pid]['non-seizure'])
    total = seiz + non
    patient_stats.append((pid, seiz, non, total))

patient_stats.sort(key=lambda x: x[3], reverse=True)

train_patients, test_patients = [], []

train_seiz = train_non = 0
test_seiz = test_non = 0

total_seiz = sum(x[1] for x in patient_stats)
total_non  = sum(x[2] for x in patient_stats)

target_train_seiz = 0.7 * total_seiz
target_train_non  = 0.7 * total_non

for pid, seiz, non, _ in patient_stats:
    if (train_seiz < target_train_seiz or train_non < target_train_non):
        train_patients.append(pid)
        train_seiz += seiz
        train_non += non
    else:
        test_patients.append(pid)
        test_seiz += seiz
        test_non += non


print(f"Train patients: {train_patients}")
print(f"Test patients: {test_patients}")


#  Building the datasets for test and train from the seizure and nonseizure data, extracting the features and segments from each other

X_train, y_train = [], []
X_test, y_test = [], []

train_feature_names = None  # we capture once

for patient_id in train_patients:
    for seg in patient_signals[patient_id]['seizure']:
        feats, names = extract_features(seg)
        X_train.append(feats)
        y_train.append(1)
        if train_feature_names is None:
            train_feature_names = names

    for seg in patient_signals[patient_id]['non-seizure']:
        feats, names = extract_features(seg)
        X_train.append(feats)
        y_train.append(0)
        if train_feature_names is None:
            train_feature_names = names


for patient_id in test_patients:
    for seg in patient_signals[patient_id]['seizure']:
        feats, _ = extract_features(seg)
        X_test.append(feats)
        y_test.append(1)

    for seg in patient_signals[patient_id]['non-seizure']:
        feats, _ = extract_features(seg)
        X_test.append(feats)
        y_test.append(0)


X_train = np.array(X_train)
X_test = np.array(X_test)
y_train = np.array(y_train)
y_test = np.array(y_test)


# Sanity check

print(f"Training set: {X_train.shape}")
print(f"Testing set: {X_test.shape}")


assert X_train.shape[1] == len(train_feature_names), "Feature mismatch detected"

Train patients: ['08', '05', '01', '10', '04', '03', '07']
Test patients: ['09', '02', '06']
Training set: (585, 460)
Testing set: (154, 460)
In [17]:
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.model_selection import GridSearchCV

param_grid = {'n_estimators': [100, 200, 300, 500]}

# Using GridSearch to choose the best n_estimators parameter
grid = GridSearchCV(
    RandomForestClassifier(random_state=42, class_weight='balanced'),
    param_grid,
    cv=5,
    scoring='f1',
    n_jobs=-1
)

grid.fit(X_train, y_train)
print(f"Best params: {grid.best_params_}")

rf = grid.best_estimator_

importances = pd.Series(rf.feature_importances_, index=train_feature_names)
print(importances.nlargest(10))

Best params: {'n_estimators': 500}
FP2-F4_D3_meanabs    0.017511
F7-T7_theta          0.015627
CZ-PZ_delta          0.014976
CZ-PZ_D1_std         0.014136
CZ-PZ_D1_energy      0.013675
T7-FT9_D2_energy     0.013093
T7-FT9_D2_std        0.012771
FT9-FT10_D3_std      0.010935
CZ-PZ_D1_meanabs     0.010805
FZ-CZ_D1_meanabs     0.009801
dtype: float64
In [18]:
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix, accuracy_score, precision_score, recall_score, f1_score, ConfusionMatrixDisplay
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def evaluate_model(model, X_test, y_test, model_name="Model"):
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    
    cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred)
    accuracy = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred)
    precision = precision_score(y_test, y_pred)
    recall = recall_score(y_test, y_pred)
    f1 = f1_score(y_test, y_pred)
    
    print(f"{model_name}:")
    print(f"Accuracy:  {accuracy:.4f}")
    print(f"Precision: {precision:.4f}")
    print(f"Recall:    {recall:.4f}")
    print(f"F1-Score:  {f1:.4f}")
    
    disp = ConfusionMatrixDisplay(confusion_matrix=cm, display_labels=['Non-Seizure', 'Seizure'])
    disp.plot(cmap='Blues')
    plt.title(f"{model_name} - Confusion Matrix")
    plt.show()


evaluate_model(rf, X_test, y_test, "Random Forest")

Random Forest:
Accuracy:  0.7403
Precision: 0.4583
Recall:    0.9706
F1-Score:  0.6226

Results¶

CNN shows very good performance on seizure detection, but the cross-validation that is done with leave-on-patient-out shows that there is very big difference between prediction performance on patients and the mean accuracy was not that great. The difference between patients is most likely due to the difference in the amount of seizure segments that each patient has. Some have 87 seizure segments while some have under 10. That has a big effect. Therefore the good performance that we first got, is over-optimistic due to problems in the data split. The model most likely learns the per-patient classification to seizure/nonseizure rather than generalising to new segments. Also the leave-one-patient-out cross-validation may not be trustworthy estimate, since there is so big difference in the amounts of segments. Maybe some other cross-validation, like k-fold, could be better estimate.

The RF doesn't show very great accuracy or precision on the problem, only 0.74 and 0.46. But in the case of seizure detection, the most importan metric is the recall. Recall tells us what fraction of the all actual seizures did the model catch. And that is the positive result that we were able to get with the model. 0.97 recall and as in the confusion matrix we can see, only one seizure out of 154 were missed. In this context this is the most important result. The goal is to reduce the amount of missed seizures, better to detect false seizures than to miss an actual one.
